lightBloom.note: SECRET classification banner at top and bottom should bleed red light into adjacent content — it dominates the real GCS visual space
Crosshair center point should have a small green bloom — the reticle emits light on a dark feed
The active TRACK phase should glow yellow, bleeding into adjacent phase bars — it is the current focus

chromaticAberration.note: None on the UI panels. But the UAV feeds themselves should have significant chromatic aberration (2-3px) — satellite-relayed video has compression artifacts

filmGrain.note: 0.04 at 30fps is specified globally. The UAV video feeds need MUCH heavier noise — 0.12 intensity with analog-static type. IR feeds (alpha/gamma) should have distinct IR sensor noise (banded horizontal lines at random intervals)

feedNoise.note: 0.08 analog-static is specified. Good but needs periodic DROPOUTS — every 60-90 frames, one random feed should have 2-3 frames of heavy static (0.4 intensity) then recover. This is satellite link degradation

crtCurvature.note: 0.02 is present. Apply ONLY to the UAV feeds, not the UI panels — real GCS uses flat-panel monitors for UI but older displays for video feeds

scanLines.note: Spacing 2 at 0.06. Good for the overall UI. But UAV feeds should have interlace artifacts — alternating-line combing effect at 0.08 opacity, especially during motion

reflectionGlare.note: GCS rooms have dim overhead lighting — faint warm gradient wash from top

irSimulation.note: IR white hot is specified for alpha/gamma. Add thermal bloom around hot objects (target bounding box area) — IR sensors bloom on heat sources. The compound should glow slightly brighter than surroundings

cameraDrift.note: Near-zero — GCS is a ground station, rock-solid. Only imperceptible HVAC vibration

dramaticReveals.note: Terminal boot with step easing is specified. Enhance: boot should show actual GCS initialization text — 'INITIALIZING SATCOM LINK... OK', 'CRYPTO LOADED... KEYED', 'GCS SOFTWARE v4.2.1... READY', then feeds connect one by one with static-to-clear transition

When JA approval comes in, the checkbox should flash green three times, the word 'APPROVED' should scale up 110% briefly then settle, and the CMD AUTH below should immediately cascade to approved too. This is the moment that changes everything

breathingAnimations.note: Crosshairs should have slow circular drift (gimbal stabilization imperfection) — the sensor ball is constantly correcting. This is what real sensor feeds look like
UAV orbit is specified. The orbit trail should be visible — a dotted circle showing the racetrack pattern behind each UAV

microInteractions.note: Degraded datalink should visibly fluctuate — the bar jitters rapidly between values. Occasionally dips to near-zero for 1-2 frames
Tracking bounding box should slightly resize as the tracker adjusts — it is not a perfect lock, the algorithm is recalculating

transitionMoments.description: Kill chain advance from TRACK to TARGET — TRACK phase fills completely with green, TARGET phase begins filling with yellow pulse, crosshair on feed_alpha tightens (zooms from x12 to x16), tracking quality upgrades from FIRM to LOCK, 9-line brief auto-populates. The mood intensifies

Legal approval cascade — JA APPROVED flips green, CMD AUTH follows 5 frames later, ROE indicator shifts but remains WEAPONS TIGHT, comms transcript shows OVERLORD: 'Cleared hot when ready'. All feeds sharpen (noise decreases 20%). The system focuses

dataDensityGaps.fix: Add MAGNETIC HEADING vs TRUE HEADING indicator, WIND ARROW overlay on each feed (small arrow showing local wind at altitude), and RECORDING indicator (red dot + 'REC' + elapsed time)
Add MGRS grid overlay at 100m resolution, elevation contour lines at 50m intervals, and ILLUMINATION ANGLE indicator for the current sun position
Add WEAPON FUZE setting (PROX/CONTACT/DELAY), TIME OF FLIGHT estimate per weapon, and COLLATERAL DAMAGE ESTIMATE radius circles on the map
Add SUNRISE/SUNSET indicator — critical for ISR operations. Format: 'SR: 05:42L / SS: 18:15L / NVG: 19:02L'
Add before/after imagery comparison — split view showing pre-strike vs post-strike satellite image thumbnail

colorGradingNotes.overallLook: Desaturated military gray-green with high-contrast data overlays — grade like Eye in the Sky (Gavin Hood). The color palette should feel COLD and clinical. Shadows at #050607 (slightly blue), highlights never pure white (cap at #D0D0D0). The RED classification banners are the only saturated color in a sea of muted tones — they should SCREAM

keyMoments.trigger: kill chain advance to TARGET phase
keyMoments.shift: Subtle overall warmth increase — tension building. Background shifts from #0A0D0F to #0C0D0F (barely perceptible red addition)
Flash white for 1 frame (weapon launch), then warm orange wash for 5 frames (rocket motor), then settle to cool blue-gray (assessment begins)

colorGradingNotes.accentUsage: UAV colors (green/cyan/orange/red) are well differentiated. But the kill chain PENDING color (#3A4450) is too similar to background — darken to #2A2E38 and add a 1px border to each pending phase box
colorGradingNotes.backgroundAtmosphere: Military panel matte is correct. Add very subtle directional lighting — a warm wash from the upper-left quadrant simulating a desk lamp that the sensor operator uses for their paper checklist

typographyNotes.fonts: Roboto Condensed is perfect for military UI — it is literally used in real defense applications. Source Code Pro for mono data is an excellent pairing
typographyNotes.monospaceReadout: Source Code Pro at weight 500 for data is clean. For HUD overlay text on video feeds, use weight 700 with 1px dark outline (text-stroke) for readability against variable video backgrounds
typographyNotes.displayFont: Feed labels at 16px Roboto Condensed 700 are appropriately visible. The classification banner should be ALL CAPS with letter-spacing: 6px for emphasis
typographyNotes.sizeHierarchy: 9px data labels are standard for military dense displays. Acceptable. But PIR checklist at secondary font weight 400 should be weight 500 — intelligence requirements must be easily scannable

continuousAmbience.sound: Constant white noise floor — satellite link static, barely audible but always present. Occasional digital chirps from the datalink modem
GCS room tone — air conditioning hum, distant keyboard clicks from the pilot station, occasional muffled radio chatter from adjacent workstations

eventSounds.sound: Short confirmatory tone — military system acknowledgment beep
Radio squelch + voice — different tone per net (COMMAND is deeper, ISR is clearer, FIRES has urgency buzz)
Decisive double-beep (high tone) — the legal checkpoint cleared. The room shifts
Intermittent digital crackling — link quality audible in the headset

silenceMoments.description: After the kill chain advances to TARGET — the room pauses. The decision weight is tangible. 5 frames of near-silence before the next comms message breaks the tension
